category_title: Children with ASD exhibiting disruptive sensory-seeking and out-of-seat behaviors
minimal_context: Matthew is joining a group of other children for a social interaction activity in a mainstream classroom setting.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Matthew, it is time to join the other children for the group activity.

STUDENT: (Approaches the group of children, focusing on a classmate who is wearing sparkly hair grips.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and find a spot to sit.

STUDENT: (Continues approaching the classmate with the sparkly hair grips instead of taking a seat.)

TUTOR: Matthew, please sit down.

STUDENT: (Touches and flicks the classmate's hair without her permission, causing her to scream and move away to avoid interacting with him.)

options.A: Respond with enthusiastic verbal praise, saying, "Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going," as he approaches the classmate.
options.B: Kneel about two feet in front of Matthew and say, "Hands out, look at the item... I want you to hold with your hands, one, two, three, look at the item, hold."
options.C: Reinforce intervals of time where Matthew does not touch the classmate's hair, providing access to a favorite toy or activity only when he successfully omits this behavior.
options.D: Provide clues and requests for clarification regarding the group activity, prompting Matthew to explain what he is supposed to be doing during this session.
